TopModule.demo_behavior:
Module: TopModule I would like you to implement a module named TopModule with the following interface. All input and output ports are one bit unless otherwise specified. - input  in            (4 bits) - output out_both      (4 bits) - output out_any       (4 bits) - output out_different (4 bits) You are given a four-bit input vector. We want to know some relationships between each bit and its neighbour: (1) out_both: Each bit of this output vector should indicate whether both the corresponding input bit and its neighbour to the left (higher index) are '1'. For example, out_both[2] should indicate if in[2] and in[3] are both 1. Since in[3] has no neighbour to the left, the answer is obvious so we don't need to know out_both[3]. (2) out_any: Each bit of this output vector should indicate whether any of the corresponding input bit and its neighbour to the right are '1'. For example, out_any[2] should indicate if either in[2] or in[1] are 1. Since in[0] has no neighbour to the right, the answer is obvious so we don't need to know out_any[0]. (3) out_different: Each bit of this output vector should indicate whether the corresponding input bit is different from its neighbour to the left. For example, out_different[2] should indicate if in[2] is different from in[3]. For this part, treat the vector as wrapping around, so in[3]'s neighbour to the left is in[0].
Key rules: Implement behavior exactly as described in the prompt.
Reset: Only as explicitly described in the prompt.
Corner cases: No additional corner cases beyond prompt requirements.
Performance: Use prompt-defined timing/latency behavior.

test_goals: Pass benchmark harness checks for all provided tests.
verification.oracle_strategy: Use benchmark-provided reference outputs as oracle.
pass_fail_criteria: DUT outputs match oracle outputs for benchmark tests.
